Strategy Description: A contrarian strategy in the futures market that buys past losers and sells past winners, filtered by trading volume and open interest.
Core Logic: Market overreaction leads to price reversals. High trading volume and low open interest are indicators of irrationality-induced inefficiencies that precede reversals.
Entry Conditions: Contracts in the high-volume, low-open interest group with the lowest (long) or greatest (short) returns in the previous week.
Indicators Used: Weekly Returns, Detrended Trading Volume, Changes in Open Interest.
Risk Management Rules: Contract weights are proportional to the difference between the contract's return and the equal-weighted average return of the group.
Long/Short Logic: Long past losers, short past winners (Contrarian).
Market Regime Suitability: Liquidity providing; often performs well during market crises but carries short-volatility risks.
Full Notes: Uses 24 types of US futures contracts. Volume is detrended by dividing by its sample mean to ensure comparability across markets.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Overreaction Hypothesis, Detrending, Median Filtering, Proportional Weighting.
